title: VONG vs. IWO: Large-Cap Stability or Small-Cap Growth Upside?
author: John Ballard
description: The article compares two growth-focused ETFs: Vanguard Russell 1000 Growth ETF (VONG), which offers lower costs and large-cap stability with a 0.06% expense ratio, and iShares Russell 2000 Growth ETF (IWO), which provides small-cap growth exposure with higher recent returns (32.4% vs 21.2% over 1 year) but a higher 0.24% expense ratio. VONG has outperformed over 5 years, while IWO has shown better recent performance, suggesting a potential market rotation toward small-cap stocks beyond the tech-dominated large-cap sector.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: VONG is presented favorably for its significantly lower expense ratio (0.06%), superior 5-year performance ($1,974 growth vs $1,257 for IWO), and lower volatility (beta 1.16 vs 1.46). It offers stability and cost efficiency for long-term investors.

IWO is viewed positively for its recent outperformance (32.4% 1-year return), more diversified sector allocation beyond tech, and attractive valuations in small-cap stocks. It represents potential upside if the bull market broadens beyond technology.
Mentioned as a top holding in IWO (3.19% weight) without specific performance commentary or investment recommendation.
Listed as a top holding in IWO (2.06% weight) without specific performance commentary or investment recommendation.

title: Tom Lee's Small-Cap Granny Shots ETF Reloads: First Solar, Zoom Among 10 New Buys
author: Chandrima Sanyal
description: Fundstrat Capital completed its May 2026 quarterly rebalance of the Fundstrat Granny Shots US Small- & Mid-Cap ETF (GRNJ), adding 10 stocks including First Solar, Zoom, Credo Technology, and Neurocrine Biosciences, while removing 8 positions. CIO Tom Lee positions the portfolio for a 'challenging but ultimately constructive' environment, with the strategy focusing on companies benefiting from structural tailwinds across themes like Millennials, Global Labor Supply, Energy, Cybersecurity, and Easing Financial Conditions.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Active rebalancing with strategic additions of quality companies across growth sectors; positioned for constructive market environment despite near-term challenges
Added to portfolio in rebalance, indicating alignment with renewable energy structural tailwinds
New addition to portfolio, likely benefiting from cybersecurity and technology themes

title: Buy The Dip? Not So Fast. 6 Stocks Investors Ignored In Last Week's Chaos
author: Erica Kollmann
description: During Friday's 1,100-point Nasdaq decline, retail traders on Robinhood showed a rotation pattern rather than a simple 'buy the dip' strategy. While mega-cap AI names like Tesla, Nvidia, and CoreWeave saw aggressive buying, major software and cloud stocks including Amazon, Microsoft, and Alphabet were net sold despite declining prices. Credo Technology was the most heavily sold stock on the platform, suggesting traders locked in gains from recent rallies rather than adding exposure to beaten-down mega-cap names.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Net seller despite market decline; buy/sell ratio well below 1.0x, indicating retail traders were exiting positions rather than buying the dip

title: Nvidia And Other 6 Chip Stocks Set For A 'Secular Upside,' Analyst Says
author: Piero Cingari
description: Bank of America analyst Vivek Arya identifies seven semiconductor stocks positioned for sustained outperformance in the AI era, driven by surging demand for AI chips and data center infrastructure. With AI data center equipment market projected to reach $1.7 trillion by 2030 (up from $264 billion in 2025), the key constraint is supply scarcity rather than demand. All seven stocks are rated Buy, with Nvidia offering the most upside at 71% to BofA's $350 price target.

title: The Anthropic IPO Could Make These 5 AI Stocks Unexpected Winners
author: Micah Zimmerman
description: Anthropic's IPO filing at a ~$1 trillion valuation will drive massive AI infrastructure spending. The company has committed $100+ billion to AWS and Google for compute capacity, positioning infrastructure suppliers like Celestica, Credo Technology, Astera Labs, Coherent, and Marvell Technology to benefit from the resulting data center build-out.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Integrates GPUs and networking gear into AI racks for hyperscalers; positioned as primary assembler for Anthropic's infrastructure expansion with recent product launches.
Supplies Active Electrical Cables for AI clusters; has three confirmed hyperscaler customers with direct Anthropic relationships, creating structural demand linkage.
Designs critical semiconductor connectivity silicon for AI racks; recently launched advanced fabric switch now shipping to leading cloud customers supporting infrastructure expansion.
Provides optical transceivers connecting data center servers; backed by $2 billion Nvidia investment and multibillion-dollar purchase commitment, positioned for optical demand growth.
Primary architect of custom silicon for hyperscalers; Nvidia CEO identified as potential trillion-dollar company; stock surged 33% on single day with strong design win pipeline.
Holds substantial equity stake in Anthropic and secured $100B+ compute commitment; positioned to benefit from infrastructure spending through AWS services and Trainium accelerators.
